summary: Unmerged closed PR improving Zebes Route stage lighting decompilation in src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c. It named route light data, replaced several anonymous literals and extern/data references with explicit typed constants/strings, added baselib lobj/debug includes, and translated grZebesRoute_8020B548 into C. The submitted checkdiff result was 99.96923%, with known remaining drift from GXColor by-value stack temp offsets.
what_changed: Added a typed grZebesRoute_LightData struct containing player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos, and a padding f32.
Defined const grZe_Route_803B83A0 as structured Zebes Route light data instead of treating it as an extern Vec3.
Added direct data/string symbols for /GrNZr.dat, grzebesroute.c, assert strings, OSReport format text, f32 lighting constants, and GXColor constants.
Replaced an OSReport call using __FILE__ and inline literals with direct references through grZe_Route_803E5E0C.fmt and grZe_Route_803E5E64.
Updated grZebesRoute_8020B42C to read grZe_Route_803B83A0.player_pos and use named f32 constants for the minimum Y clamp and zero X assignment.

Translated grZebesRoute_8020B548, the stage light setup routine, using HSD_GObjGXLinkHead[4], HSD_LObj traversal, light type checks, LOBJ_SPOT/LOBJ_POINT handling, scaled Vec3 positions/interests, colors, spot parameters, and distance attenuation.
Added includes for baselib/debug.h and baselib/lobj.h to support __assert and HSD_LObj APIs.
smart_moves: Promoted the previously opaque grZe_Route_803B83A0 data into a stage-specific struct, which made later lighting code readable and gave each Vec3 field semantic meaning.
Kept target data references explicit with grZe_Route_* symbols for strings, colors, and float constants, helping align generated code with the original binary's data layout.
Factored repeated light setup constants into named const objects rather than relying on inline literals, improving both matching control and future readability.
Used current light position and LOBJ_TYPE_MASK to distinguish the spotlight from upper and lower point lights, matching behavior without inventing higher-level abstractions beyond the evidence.
Documented the remaining match issue precisely: GXColor by-value stack temporary offsets differ while surrounding control flow and Vec3 stack slots align.
decomp_lessons: For stage lighting routines, first recover the static data layout: positions, interests, colors, attenuation constants, and string/assert data often need to be named before the function becomes tractable.
When a data symbol initially appears as a Vec3, inspect neighboring constant data; it may actually be the first field of a larger stage-specific struct.
Direct references to file names, assert strings, and OSReport format strings can matter for matching and should be represented as data symbols when the target uses them.
GXColor passed by value can create stubborn stack-slot drift even when the logic is otherwise very close; isolate that drift in the PR notes instead of obscuring the translated routine.
Adding small dummy padding such as int pad[3] may be a matching tactic for stack layout, but this PR still had known GXColor stack offset drift, so the tactic was not a complete fix here.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific static symbols followed the existing grZe_Route_<address> pattern, such as grZe_Route_803B83A0 and grZe_Route_804DB920.
The recovered light data type used a descriptive stage-prefixed name: grZebesRoute_LightData.
Struct fields used semantic snake_case names tied to usage: player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos.
Function names remained address-based for unmatched/decompiled routines, e.g. grZebesRoute_8020B548.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Verification command reported in PR body: python configure.py && ninja.
Function-level checkdiff command reported: python tools/checkdiff.py grZebesRoute_8020B548 --no-tty --format json, yielding 99.96923%.
Used explicit const f32 and GXColor symbols to force data references instead of local inline constants.
Used a typed aggregate for grZe_Route_803B83A0 so member loads could represent multiple related Vec3 constants from one data block.
Added a local int pad[3] in the lower point light branch, apparently as a stack-layout/matching aid, though the PR notes say GXColor stack temp offsets still drifted.
Known remaining drift was specifically GXColor by-value stack temp offsets at 8/12/16 versus target 20/24/28.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR was closed without merge according to the context; no explicit reason for closure is included in the provided evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c or grZebesRoute_8020B548 to see whether the GXColor stack offset drift was resolved.
Search for other stage lighting routines using HSD_LObj traversal and GXColor by-value calls for reusable matching patterns.
Check whether grZebesRoute_LightData or equivalent field names were accepted in a merged successor PR.
Investigate why PR #2494 was closed unmerged if external comments or later commits are available outside this slice.
